$comment: Tools & resources surfaced on /resources. Edit this file, git push, deploy = updated page. kind: 'affiliate' | 'free' | 'paid'. `ref` is where the affiliate/ref param lives — swap the `affiliateUrl` directly with whatever the vendor gives you. Always keep the plain `url` (un-tagged) so we can render a 'direct link' fallback if needed.
disclosure: Some links on this page are affiliate links. If you use them, BuyOrBeSold may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. We only list things we actually use or have vetted ourselves. Not financial advice.

categories.name: Bullion — Silver, Gold, Platinum
categories.blurb: Where I buy physical metal. I size based on real yields and dollar direction, not headlines. If you're new to bullion, start with sovereign coins (ASE, Maple, Eagle) before bars — liquidity matters more than spread.

items.name: JM Bullion
items.blurb: My primary dealer for silver + gold. Fast shipping, insured, tight spreads on Silver Eagles and fractional gold. Their price alerts are actually useful.
items.bestFor: Silver Eagles, Gold Maples, fractional gold (1/10 oz)

categories.name: Brokers & Trading Platforms
categories.blurb: What I use to execute. Commissions matter less than fills and outage history. The scanner pairs best with a broker that gives you hot-keys and level 2.

items.name: Alpaca
items.blurb: Commission-free stocks with a solid API. What BuyOrBeSold connects to for broker-verified traders. Paper trading is free and realistic.
items.bestFor: API trading, paper testing, verified journal

categories.name: Charting & Screening
categories.blurb: Charts and screens I pull up every session. The scanner on this site handles my day-trade filter — but these are where I zoom in once a name flags.

items.name: TradingView
items.blurb: Best charts on the internet. Free tier covers most of what you need. Paid tier unlocks multi-chart layouts + extra indicators.

items.name: Finviz
items.blurb: Fastest way to get a snapshot of a ticker — fundamentals, technicals, ownership, short interest on one page. I start every new-name deep-dive here.

categories.name: Data & News
categories.blurb: Where the numbers and headlines actually come from. You don't need a Bloomberg terminal — you need one good data feed and a plan.

items.name: Finnhub
items.blurb: Free-tier market data API I use for float data, economic calendar, and company news. Paid tiers unlock WebSockets + pre/post-market.
items.bestFor: Float lookup, economic calendar, news feeds

items.name: Yahoo Finance
items.blurb: Free delayed quotes, historical data, earnings calendar. Not real-time but reliable for EOD analysis and backtests.
items.bestFor: Historical data, earnings calendar, basic screeners

categories.name: Reading & Research
categories.blurb: The writing that made me a better trader. None of these will make you money directly — they shape how you think about risk, cycles, and positioning.
